behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: local module handoff between evaluate-stage logic and artifact persistence keying. The failure is incorrect slot routing (`grounding` vs `plan`), which appears at the command->artifact-store boundary. Pass 2 mapping: Integration is the minimum. Rejected Unit because this behavior is about an interaction side effect (what is persisted), not just pure return-value logic. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because the behavior text only requires artifact-key correctness and does not require real OS/network/subprocess, real model judgment, or multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage with an in-process artifact store fixture and assert exactly one new/updated `grounding` artifact is written while `plan` is unchanged by this stage.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: implement flow must read one artifact slot and write another through local module wiring. Failure is cross-module data flow (artifact reader + implement logic + writer). Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Rejected Unit because a pure function test would miss wiring mistakes in artifact retrieval/persistence. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about local artifact IO semantics, not real infrastructure, model-quality dependence, or multi-agentic chains.
behaviors.test_description: Seed artifact store with `grounding`, execute implement-stage, assert implement consumed grounding context and wrote execution output to `plan` while preserving `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: conditional control flow plus side-effect suppression at the deep-dive->artifact-write boundary. Real failure is unwanted write invocation under a specific precondition (diagnosis already present). Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Rejected Unit because mocking internal branches alone can miss actual persistence side effects. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text disqualifies higher levels: it is a local side-effect prevention rule, with no required real OS/network/model/pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue content containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage with artifact store spy/fixture, and assert `plan` is not overwritten (or not written) while other expected outputs remain intact.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: end-to-end propagation across multiple agentic stages in sequence (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`) and final output reflects earlier-stage grounding. Behavior text explicitly requires a full multi-step agent run, which is a pipeline-level failure boundary. Pass 2 mapping: Workflow minimum. Rejected Agentic as too low because a single model call cannot expose cross-stage propagation regressions. Rejected System as too low because subprocess/OS realism alone does not cover multi-step agent pipeline correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real three-stage pipeline in order, capture artifacts after each stage, and assert final `plan` content incorporates signals from the earlier `grounding` artifact generated during evaluate/write-plan stage.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1 dependency: implement-stage interaction with artifact store under missing-input condition plus safe fallback write behavior. Failure boundary is module wiring/state handling, not pure algorithmic output. Pass 2 mapping: Integration. Rejected Unit because corruption/non-corruption of stored artifacts is a persistence interaction concern. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text concerns local fallback semantics with absent artifact, not real infra, model quality, or multi-agentic chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement-stage with no `grounding` present and pre-existing unrelated artifacts, assert a valid `plan` is produced, fallback path is used, and unrelated artifacts remain unchanged.
